name: Tornavacas - Los Pilones via the cherry blossom route – Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve (PR-CC 2)
name_original: Tornavacas - Los Pilones por la ruta del cerezo en flor – Reserva Natural Garganta de los Infiernos (PR-CC 2)
region_intro: Hiking around Tornavacas offers access to the Jerte Valley and the Sierra de Gredos mountain range, characterized by diverse natural landscapes. The region features picturesque mountains, clear rivers, and numerous waterfalls, with lower elevations covered by forests of oak and chestnut. Higher up, the terrain transitions to rugged, glacially sculpted landscapes with rocky slopes and alpine meadows. This area provides a rich network of trails through varied environments. Best hiking trails around Tornavacas The most popular hiking route is Ruta del Trabuquete and Antiguo Camino de Castilla Loop, a 8.0 miles (12.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 57 minutes to complete. This moderate loop offers mountain views and passes by waterfalls. Another top favourite among local hikers is Laguna del Duque Trail, a moderate 3.2 miles (5.1 km) path. This route leads to the largest glacial lake in the Sierra de Gredos, providing forest and mountain vistas. Local hikers also love the Tornavacas - Los Pilones via the cherry blossom route – Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve (PR-CC 2), a 14.9 miles (23.9 km) trail leading through cherry orchards and the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve, often completed in about 7 hours 7 minutes. Hiking around Tornavacas is defined by mountain ranges, river valleys, and glacial formations. The extensive network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to challenging high-altitude hikes. The routes in Tornavacas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Tornavacas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there around Tornavacas?
A: There are over 100 hiking routes around Tornavacas, offering a wide variety of experiences. These trails explore the Jerte Valley and the Sierra de Gredos mountain range, featuring diverse landscapes from river valleys to high-altitude glacial formations.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Tornavacas?
A: The Jerte Valley, where Tornavacas is located, is particularly famous for its spectacular cherry blossoms in spring, making it a beautiful time to visit. Waterfalls and cascades are especially impressive after rainfall. The Sierra de Gredos offers stunning alpine meadows in summer and dramatic landscapes year-round, though higher elevations can be challenging in winter.

Q: Are there any trails with waterfalls or natural pools?
A: Yes, the region is rich in water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990788410/tornavacas-los-pilones-via-the-cherry-blossom-route-garganta-de-los-infiernos-natural-reserve-pr-cc-2" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tornavacas - Los Pilones via the cherry blossom route&lt;/a&gt; leads into the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve, famous for its hydrological network and the iconic 'Los Pilones' giant potholes. You can also find waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/874276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Trabuquete&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5701178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Chorrera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of difficulty levels can I expect on Tornavacas hiking trails?
A: Tornavacas offers a range of trails suitable for various fitness levels. Approximately 16% of the routes are easy, 58% are moderate, and 26% are difficult. This means you can find everything from gentle walks through valleys to challenging high-altitude hikes in the Sierra de Gredos.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Tornavacas?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, the region offers easy walks suitable for families. Look for trails with lower elevation gain and shorter distances. The natural beauty of the Jerte Valley, with its rivers and forests, provides an engaging environment for children to explore.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Tornavacas?
A: Many trails in natural areas like the Jerte Valley and Sierra de Gredos are generally dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas like the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes near Tornavacas?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990325650/ruta-del-trabuquete-and-antiguo-camino-de-castilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Trabuquete and Antiguo Camino de Castilla Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 12.9 km trail that offers mountain views and passes by waterfalls. Another challenging loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1141306212/canchal-de-la-ceja-and-laguna-del-duque-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canchal de la Ceja and Laguna del Duque Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints to see while hiking?
A: Hikers can explore several significant natural landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380425898/laguna-del-duque-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Duque Trail&lt;/a&gt; leads to the largest glacial lake in the Sierra de Gredos. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5638307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrero de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for spectacular views, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4297239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Puerto de Tornavacas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the trailheads in Tornavacas?
A: While Tornavacas is accessible by public transport, direct public transport to specific trailheads within the Jerte Valley or Sierra de Gredos can be limited. It's often best to plan for a combination of public transport to Tornavacas itself and then consider local taxis or personal vehicles for reaching more remote starting points.

Q: Do I need any permits for hiking in the Tornavacas area?
A: Generally, permits are not required for day hiking on established trails in the Tornavacas area, including the Jerte Valley and parts of the Sierra de Gredos. However, for specific activities or access to certain protected zones, it's always recommended to check with local tourism offices or park authorities for the most current regulations.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Tornavacas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 370 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse natural landscapes, from the picturesque Jerte Valley with its cherry orchards and waterfalls to the rugged, glacially sculpted terrain of the Sierra de Gredos. The extensive network of trails catering to various ability levels is also a highlight.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay in Tornavacas after a hike?
A: Tornavacas, as a village, offers local amenities including accommodation options like guesthouses and rural hotels, as well as cafes and restaurants where hikers can refuel and relax after their adventures. These establishments often serve traditional Extremaduran cuisine.
tips: This impressive natural setting is one of those that remain stuck in the retina. With no more and no less than 13 natural pools, this incredible ravine shows off curious rock formations, the result of the action of water. It could be said that it is the heart of the Garganta de los Infiernos nature reserve.

This picturesque and colorful route runs through the valley of the Jerte River between Tornavacas and the town of Jerte, passing through the Pilones and the Garganta de los Infiernos nature reserve. It is advisable to do it during the cherry blossom weeks, between the end of March and the beginning of April, when you can appreciate the beautiful landscape worthy of a postcard.
